SPRING MILL HEALTH CAMPUS

CCN: 155764 · MERRILLVILLE, IN 46410 · Lake County

Overview

Address
101 W 87TH AVE, MERRILLVILLE, IN 46410
Phone

Certified beds

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 23.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-09-10 12 11 1 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-11 22 11 11 108 1 108
Total weighted health score
63.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (48)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

46 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-07-01 2025-07-15
0620 Not require residents to give up Medicare or Medicaid benefits, or pay privately as a condition of admission; and must tell residents what care they do not provide. D 2025-02-18 2025-03-03
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-02-18 2025-03-03
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2024-09-10 2024-10-01
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-02-20 2024-03-12
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-02-20 2024-03-12
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-02-20 2024-03-12
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-02-20 2024-03-12
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-02-20 2024-03-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-02-20 2024-03-12
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-09-19 2023-09-27
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0773 Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. D 2023-08-11 2023-08-28
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-06-21 2023-06-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2023-06-21 2023-06-09
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. G 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-06-27 2022-07-15

Methodology & sources

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
